summary: The EU is forcing Google to share search data and open up AI on Android, while xAI is suing users for generating CSAM with its Grok chatbot. Hyundai workers are striking over humanoid robots, and Linus Torvalds is ignoring critics of AI coding in Linux. Enterprises are struggling with AI security and compute gaps, and New York's governor is using AI to analyze state rules.
insight: The increasing use of AI in various industries is leading to a rise in concerns over security, privacy, and job displacement, highlighting the need for responsible AI development and deployment.
highlights: Google faces EU regulations on search data and AI on Android
xAI sues users for generating CSAM with its Grok chatbot
Hyundai workers strike over humanoid robots in US factories

articles.title: It's official: EU will force Google to share search data and open up AI on Android
articles.description: Google says these changes could endanger user privacy and security.

articles.title: xAI can’t deny Grok makes CSAM anymore. So it’s suing users.
articles.description: Elon Musk's xAI files first lawsuit against Grok user accused of making child sex images.

articles.title: Fear of humanoid robots spurs human workers to strike at Hyundai auto factory
articles.description: Hyundai aims to deploy 25,000 Atlas robots starting with US factories in 2028.

articles.title: Linus Torvalds to critics of AI coding in Linux: "Fork it. Or just walk away."
articles.description: Creator says he will "very loudly ignore" those arguing for a ban on AI tools.
